records.title: Journal
records.summary: Shortlist venues by matching manuscript type, field norms, review speed, and submission constraints before formatting work begins.
records.desc: Venue triage entrypoint for researchers who need a faster way to narrow candidate journals instead of browsing endlessly.
records.usageGuide: Start with field, manuscript type, constraints, and your non-negotiables. Use it early, then revisit after the paper stabilizes.
records.outcome: A smaller, defensible venue shortlist with explicit tradeoffs.

records.taskLine: Are the references and claims still defensible?
records.title: Cite Check
records.summary: Run a focused verification pass on references, quotations, and support claims before submission or publication.
records.desc: Citation integrity entrypoint for catching broken support chains, weak references, and quote drift before they become reviewer problems.
records.usageGuide: Use near the end of drafting or after a major revision. Prioritize the abstract, introduction, results claims, and any sentence carrying strong evidence language.
audience: editors doing a final evidence pass
records.outcome: A cleaner manuscript with obvious reference risks surfaced before reviewers do.

records.taskLine: How do we turn reading into reusable knowledge?
records.title: PaperDeck
records.summary: Convert papers, notes, and takeaways into reusable cards that preserve method, finding, and relevance in a compact format.
records.desc: Knowledge capture entrypoint for people who read a lot but lose the useful parts because nothing gets normalized into a repeatable note structure.
records.usageGuide: Use after reading a paper worth keeping. Capture the question, method, key result, and why it matters to your own work.
records.outcome: A reusable card deck that makes later synthesis and briefing much easier.

records.taskLine: What analysis path actually fits the question?
records.title: Stats
records.summary: Guide the user from research question and data shape to a narrower analysis path, with method caveats stated plainly.
records.desc: Method selection entrypoint for users who need a practical route through statistical options rather than a long undifferentiated list.
records.usageGuide: Start with the research question, variable types, sample structure, and the decision you need to make. Use it before running software menus blindly.
records.outcome: A smaller set of defensible analysis routes with assumptions visible up front.
